 canvas . 

 in a very few minute Melville recommence conversation , - no more 
 reference to Lily than if she have never exist . " yes , my last picture 
 have be indeed a success , - a reward complete , if tardy , for all the 
 bitterness of former struggle make in vain , for the gall sense of 
 injustice , the anguish of which only an artist know , when unworthy 
 rival be rank before he . 


  " ' foe quick to blame , and friend afraid to praise . ' 


 " true that I have still much to encounter ; the clique still seek to 
 disparage I , but between I and the clique there stand at last the 
 giant form of the public , and at last critic of graver weight than the 
 clique have deign to accord to I a high rank than even the public 
 yet acknowledge . ah , Mr. Chillingly , you do not profess to be a judge of 
 painting , but , excuse I , just look at this letter . I receive it 
 only last night from the great connoisseur of my art , certainly in 
 England , perhaps in Europe . " here Melville draw , from the side - pocket 
 of his picturesque moyen age surtout , a letter sign by a name 
 authoritative to all who , be painter themselves , acknowledge 
 authority in one who could no more paint a picture himself than Addison , 
 the able critic of the great poem modern Europe have produce , could 
 have write ten line of the " Paradise Lost , " and thrust the letter 
 into Kenelm 's hand . Kenelm read it listlessly , with an increase 
 contempt for an artist who could so find in gratified vanity consolation 
 for the life go from earth . but , listlessly as he read the letter , the 
 sincere and fervent enthusiasm of the laudatory content impress he , 
 and the preeminent authority of the signature could not be deny . 

 the letter be write on the occasion of Melville 's recent election to 
 the dignity of R. A. , successor to a very great artist whose death have 
 create a vacancy in the Academy . he return the letter to Melville , 
 say , " this be the letter I see you read last night as I look 
 in at your window . indeed , for a man who care for the opinion of other 
 man , this letter be very flattering ; and for the painter who care for 
 money , it must be very pleasant to know by how many guineas every inch 
 of his canvas may be cover . " unable long to control his passion of 
 rage , of scorn , of agonizing grief , Kenelm then burst forth : " man , man , 
 whom I once accept as a teacher on human life , - a teacher to warm , to 
 brighten , to exalt mine own indifferent , dreamy , slow - pulse self ! have 
 not the one woman whom thou didst select out of this overcrowded world 
 to be bone of thy bone , flesh of thy flesh , vanish evermore from the 
 earth , - little more than a year since her voice be silence , her heart 
 cease to beat ? but how slight be such loss to thy life compare to the 
 worth of a compliment that flatter thy vanity ! " 

 the artist rise to his foot with an indignant impulse . but the angry 
 flush fade from his cheek as he look on the countenance of his 
 rebuker . he walk up to he , and attempt to take his hand , but Kenelm 
 snatch it scornfully from his grasp . 

 " poor friend , " say Melville , sadly and soothingly , " I do not think you 
 love she thus deeply . pardon I . " he draw a chair close to Kenelm 's , 
 and after a brief pause go on thus , in very earnest tone , " I be not 
 so heartless , not so forgetful of my loss as you suppose . but reflect , 
 you have but just learn of her death , you be under the first shock of 
 grief . More than a year have be give to I for gradual submission to 
 the decree of Heaven . now listen to I , and try to listen calmly . I 
 be many year old than you : I ought to know well the condition 
 on which man hold the tenure of life . life be composite , many - side : 
 nature do not permit it to be lastingly monopolize by a single 
 passion , or while yet in the prime of its strength to be lastingly 
 blight by a single sorrow . survey the great mass of our common race , 
 engage in the various calling , some the humblest , some the lofty , 
 by which the business of the world be carry on , - can you justly 
 despise as heartless the poor trader , or the great statesman , when it 
 may be but a few day after the loss of some one nearest and dear to 
 his heart , the trader reopen his shop , the statesman reappear in his 
 office ? but in I , the votary of art , in I you behold but the weakness 
 of gratified vanity ; if I feel joy in the hope that my art may triumph , 
 and my country may add my name to the list of those who contribute to 
 her renown , where and when ever live an artist not sustain by that 
 hope , in privation , in sickness , in the sorrow he must share with his 
 kind ? nor be this hope that of a feminine vanity , a sickly craving for 
 applause ; it identify itself with glorious service to our land , to 
 our race , to the child of all after time . our art can not triumph , our 
 name can not live , unless we achieve a something that tend to beautify 
 or ennoble the world in which we accept the common heritage of toil and 
 of sorrow , in order therefrom to work out for successive multitude a 
 recreation and a joy . " 

 while the artist thus speak Kenelm lift towards his face eye charge 
 with suppress tear . and the face , kindling as the artist vindicate 
 himself from the young man 's bitter charge , become touchingly sweet in 
 its grave expression at the close of the not ignoble defence . 

 " Enough , " say Kenelm , rise . " there be a ring of truth in what you 
 say . I can conceive the artist 's , the poet 's escape from this world , 
 when all therein be death and winter , into the world he create and 
 colour at his will with the hue of summer . so , too , I can conceive 
 how the man whose life be sternly fit into the groove of a trader 's 
 calling , or a statesman 's duty , be bear on by the force of custom , 
 afar from such brief halting - spot as a grave . but I be no poet , no 
 artist , no trader , no statesman ; I have no calling , my life be fix 
 into no groove . Adieu . " 

 " hold a moment . not now , but somewhat later , ask yourself whether any 
 life can be permit to wander in space , a monad detach from the 
 life of other . into some groove or other , soon or later , it 
 must settle , and be bear on obedient to the law of Nature and the 
 responsibility to God . " 



 CHAPTER XIII 

 KENELM go back alone , and with downcast look , through the desolate , 
 flowerless garden , when at the other side of the gate a light touch be 
 lay on his arm . he look up , and recognize Mrs. Cameron . 

 " I see you , " she say , " from my window come to the house , and I have 
 be wait for you here . I wish to speak to you alone . allow I to 
 walk beside you . " ' 

 Kenelm incline his head assentingly , but make no answer . they be 
 nearly midway between the cottage and the burial - ground when Mrs. 
 Cameron resume , her tone quick and agitated , contrast her habitual 
 languid quietude , - 

 " I have a great weight on my mind ; it ought not to be remorse . I act

 err , - if I judge wrongly , do say you at least forgive I . " she seize 
 his hand , press it convulsively . Kenelm mutter inaudibly : a sort

 Cameron go on , - 

 " you could not have marry Lily ; you know you could not . the secret of

 " no , - no ; the secret must have come out . the cruel world would have

 assent , apparently without a pang , to become his wife ; and she seem

 deceive . from the day you see she last , she begin to fade away ; but

 she mourn . she never own to I that it be your , - never till too

 " her wish ? do you think I could disobey it ? I know not if I have 
 anything to forgive . if I have , now could I not forgive one who love 
 she ? God comfort we both . " 

 he bend down and kiss Mrs. Cameron 's forehead . the poor woman throw

 when she have recover her emotion , she say , - 

 " and now , it be with so much light a heart that I can fulfil her

 make I one promise ? never tell Melville how she love you . she be so 
 careful he should never guess that . and if he know it be the thought of

 " you would not ask such a promise if you could guess how sacred from all

 Mrs. Cameron place a letter in Kenelm 's hand , and murmur in accent 
 break by a sob , " she give it to I the day before her last , " leave he ,

 now understand he , at last , too well not to feel that on open that 
 letter he must be alone with the dead . 

 it be strange that we need have so little practical household knowledge 
 of each other to be in love . never till then have Kenelm 's eye rest 
 upon Lily 's handwriting . and he now gaze at the formal address on the 
 envelope with a sort of awe . unknown handwriting come to he from an

 he turn the envelope over and over , - not impatiently , as do the

 he be now so near the burial - ground . where should the first letter 
 ever receive from she - the sole letter he ever could receive - be so 
 reverentially , lovingly read , as at her grave ? 

 he walk on to the burial - ground , sit down by the grave , break the 
 envelope ; a poor little ring , with a poor little single turquoise ,

 word , - 


 the ring come back to you . I could not live to marry another . I never 
 know how I love you - till , till I begin to pray that you might not love 
 I too much . darling ! darling ! good - by , darling ! 

  LILY . 

 do not   let Lion ever see this , or ever know what it say to you . he be so 
 good , and deserve to be so happy . do you remember the day of the ring ? 
 Darling ! darling ! 



 CHAPTER XIV . 

 somewhat more than another year have roll away . it be early spring 
 in London . the tree in the park and square be bud into leaf and 
 blossom . Leopold Travers have have a brief but serious conversation with 
 his daughter , and now go forth on horseback . handsome and graceful 
 still , Leopold Travers when in London be pleased to find himself

 Cecilia be in the small drawing - room , which be exclusively

 LADY GLENALVON . - " I own , my dear , dear Cecilia , that I arrange myself at 
 last on the side of your father . how earnestly at one time I have hop

 which make my father speak to I - for the first time - almost sternly . "

 CECILIA . - " command I to reconsider my rejection of it . he have

 LADY GLENALVON . - " so he have I . of course you might choose among other 
 candidate for your hand one of much high worldly rank , of much large

 love ! " 

 " but it be not you whom he love . " 

 " something here , something at my heart , tell I that he will love I 
 yet ; and , if not , I be content to be his friend . " 



 CHAPTER XV . 

 while the conversation just related take place between Cecilia and

 it be true that Chillingly Gordon have , within the very short space of 
 time that have elapse since his entrance into the House of Commons , 
 achieve one of those reputation which mark out a man for early 
 admission into the progressive career of office , - not a very showy 
 reputation , but a very solid one . he have none of the gift of the 
 genuine orator , no enthusiasm , no imagination , no imprudent burst of 
 fiery word from a passionate heart . but he have all the gift of

 readiness for extempore reply ; industry and method for prepared

 it be a great occasion . "

 " at his expense ? decidedly not . though his office compel he to

 Kenelm have come back ? "

 parson 's son , - or why not to yourself ? I often think that for the prize

 CHAPTER the last . 

 it be a field night in the House of Commons , - an adjourn debate , 
 open by George Belvoir , who have be , the last two year , very slowly

 more than justify Kenelm 's prediction of his career . heir to a noble 
 name and vast estate , extremely hard - work , very well informed , it 
 be impossible that he should not creep on . that night he speak sensibly

 listen to courteously , and greet with a faint " hear , hear ! " of

 then the House gradually thin till nine o'clock , at which hour it 
 become very rapidly crowd . a Cabinet minister have solemnly rise ,

 include a corpulent blue - book . lean his arm on the red box , he

 " Sir , I join issue with the right honourable gentleman opposite . he

 up rise a crowd of eager face , from which the Speaker , as previously 
 arrange with the party whip , select one , - a young face , hardy , 
 intelligent , emotionless . 

 I need not say that it be the face of Chillingly Gordon . his position

 he habitually support the government ; his speech have be hitherto

 difference be know to the chief of the Opposition , and hence the

 ten o'clock , and for the first time in reply to a Cabinet minister . 
 it be a position in which a young party man make or mar his future . 
 chillingly Gordon speak from the third row behind the government ; he have 
 be duly caution by Mivers not to affect a conceited independence , or

 himself below the gangway . speak thus , amid the rank and file of the 
 ministerial supporter , any opinion at variance with the mouthpiece of 
 the Treasury Bench would be sure to produce a more effective sensation 
 than if deliver from the rank of the mutinous Bashi Bazouks divide 
 by the gangway from well discipline force . his first brief sentence 
 enthral the House , conciliate the ministerial side , keep the 
 Opposition side in suspense . the whole speech be , indeed , felicitously 
 adroit , and especially in this , that , while in opposition to the 
 Government as a whole , it express the opinion of a powerful section

 enamoured of a New Idea , the progress of the age would probably render 
 a safe investment for the confidence which honest Gordon repose in its 
 chance of beat its colleague . 

 it be not , however , till Gordon have conclude that the cheer of his

 could get that man . " the Cabinet minister whom Gordon have answer - more

 two gentleman in the Speaker 's gallery , who have sit there from the

 of the two gentleman issue from the Speaker 's gallery , accost he

 " ha ! chillingly , how be you ? do not know you be in town . be

 " I like yours much well . " 

 " mine ! " cry George , very much flattered and very much surprised . " oh , 
 mine be a mere humdrum affair , a plain statement of the reason for the 
 vote I should give . and Gordon 's be anything but that . you do not like

 " I do not   know what his opinion be . but I do not like his idea . " 

 " I do not   quite understand you . what idea ? " 

 " the new one ; by which it be show how rapidly a great state can be 
 make small . " 

 here Mr. Belvoir be take aside by a brother member , on an important 
 matter to be bring before the committee on salmon fishery , on which

 " it tell much for the duration of a people when it accord with the

 cent ! ' " 

 pleased with his own unwonted animation , and with the sympathizing

 success in Parliament , Gordon 's suit to Cecilia Travers , favour , as

 generation to which the destiny of those to follow be intrust ;

 association which an english public man connect with an English home :

 aspiration , of a life that must go through labour to achieve renown ;

 his son do not interrupt he by a word , Sir Peter in his eagerness not

 and have now make halt in the middle of Westminster bridge , bend 
 over the massive parapet and gaze abstractedly upon the wave of 
 the starlit river . on the right the stately length of the people 's 
 legislative palace , so new in its date , so elaborately in each detail 
 ancient in its form , stretch on towards the lowly and jagged roof of 
 penury and crime . well might these be so near to the hall of a people 's 
 legislative palace : near to the heart of every legislator for a people 
 must be the mighty problem how to increase a people 's splendour and its 
 virtue , and how to diminish its penury and its crime . 

 " how strange it be , " say Kenelm , still bend over the parapet , 
 " that throughout all my desultory wandering I have ever be attract 
 towards the sight and the sound of running water , even those of the 
 humblest rill ! of what thought , of what dream , of what memory , 
 colour the history of my past , the wave of the humblest rill could 
 speak , be the wave themselves not such supreme philosopher , - rouse 
 indeed on their surface , vex by a check to their own course , but so 
 indifferent to all that make gloom or death to the mortal who think 
 and dream and feel beside their bank . " 

 " bless I , " say Peter to himself , " the boy have get back to his old vein 
 of humour and melancholy . he have not hear a word I have be say . 
 Travers be right . he will never do anything in life . why do I christen 
 him Kenelm ? he might as well have be christen Peter . " still , loth 
 to own that his eloquence have be expend in vain and that the wish of 
 his heart be doom to expire disappoint , Sir Peter say aloud , " you 
 have not listen to what I say ; Kenelm , you grieve I . " 

 " grieve you ! you ! do not say that , Father , dear Father . listen to you ! 
 every word you have say have sink into the deep deep of my heart . 
 pardon my foolish , purposeless snatch of talk to myself : it be but my 
 way , only my way , dear Father ! " 

 " boy , boy , " cry Sir Peter , with tear in his voice , " if you could 
 get out of those odd way of yours I should be so thankful . but if 
 you can not , nothing you can do shall grieve I . only , let I say this ; 
 running water have have a great charm for you . with a humble rill you 
 associate thought , dream , memorie in your past . but now you halt by 
 the stream of the mighty river : before you the senate of an empire wide 
 than Alexander 's ; behind you the market of a commerce to which that of 
 Tyre be a pitiful trade . look far down , those squalid hovel , 
 how much there to redeem or to remedy ; and out of sight , but not very 
 distant , the nation 's Walhalla , ' victory or Westminster Abbey ! ' the 
 humble rill have witness your past . have the mighty river no effect on 
 your future ? the rill keep no record of your past : shall the river 
 keep no record of your future ? ah , boy , boy , I see you be dream 
 still , - no use talk . let we go home . " 

 " I be not dream , I be tell myself that the time have come to 
 replace the old Kenelm with the new idea , by a new Kenelm with the 
 Ideas of Old . ah ! perhaps we must , - at whatever cost to ourselves , - we 
 must go through the romance of life before we clearly detect what be 
 grand in its reality . I can no long lament that I stand estranged 
 from the object and pursuit of my race . I have learn how much I have 
 with they in common . I have know love ; I have know sorrow . " 

 Kenelm pause a moment , only a moment , then lift the head which , 
 during that pause , have droop , and stand erect at the full height of 
 his stature , startle his father by the change that have pass over his 
 face ; lip , eye , his whole aspect , eloquent with a resolute enthusiasm , 
 too grave to be the flash of a pass moment . 

 " ay , ay , " he say , " victory or Westminster Abbey ! the world be a 
 battle - field in which the bad wound be the deserter , stricken as 
 they seek to fly , and hush the groan that would betray the secret of 
 their inglorious hiding - place . the pain of wound receive in the thick 
 of the fight be scarcely feel in the joy of service to some honour 
 cause , and be amply atone by the reverence for noble scar . my choice 
 be make . not that of deserter , that of soldier in the rank . " 

 " it will not be long before you rise from the rank , my boy , if you hold 
 fast to the Idea of Old , symbolize in the English battle - cry , ' victory 
 or Westminster Abbey . ' " 

 so say , Sir Peter take his son 's arm , lean on it proudly ; and so , 
 into the crowded thoroughfare , from the halting - place on the modern 
 bridge that span the legendary river , pass the Man of the Young 
 Generation to fate beyond the verge of the horizon to which the eye of 
 my generation must limit their wistful gaze . 



 the END .
